methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

data.narrative:
Rocket Factory Augsburg is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $80M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $80M raised proxy, implying a $152M valuation. The peer-median approach benchmarks against three same-sector, same-stage private comparables: Cowboy Space ($2.0B), Aalyria ($1.3B), and Hubble Network ($400M). This yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied given the absence of target revenue disclosure, though the sector median P/S multiple is 36.9x across the public set.

The two applicable methods produce a material divergence. Triangulation across both approaches supports a range of $129M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $726M. The wide spread reflects stage-progression conservatism versus peer-median volatility driven by high dispersion in comparable company valuations ($400M to $2.0B). Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three private peers—particularly whether Cowboy Space at $2.0B reflects comparable development stage and commercialization risk—and the absence of revenue data to anchor multiples-based analysis.
